title: Blauer Schnipsel
subtitle: Co-generating a social fabric
description:
The Blauer Schnipsel project is a series of graphic-tailoring upcycling workshops, documented in a publication designed to narrate and replicate this social experiment. With over 70 participants, we transformed textile waste—specifically, the cut-offs from the production of the Blauer Schurz (the traditional South Tyrolean apron)—into 54 modular decorated textiles, covering a total area of 27 square meters.

This waste became the raw material for collective making. The workshops generated a practical methodology and a set of tools now fully described in our publication "Blauer Schnipsel. Co-generating a Social Fabric".

Beyond aesthetics, Blauer Schnipsel proposes a political gesture: reclaiming the symbolic weight of a garment historically tied to local identity and exclusion, and reimagining it as a catalyst for intergenerational, intercultural, and participatory creation. The act of sewing—often dismissed or feminized—was repositioned as a form of commoning, transforming leftovers into decoration for public space.
details: Special thanks to all the participants, Weight Station, Franzlab, Fablab Bitz, Officine Vispa, Daria Akimenko, Secil Ugur Yavuz, Aart van Bezooijen, Barbara Stocker.
